The error is a logical fallacy: from \( b^2 \equiv \pm 1 \pmod{p} \), one cannot conclude that \(-1\) is a quadratic residue; it only follows if the specific congruence is \( b^2 \equiv -1 \). The step fails to consider the case where \( b^2 \equiv 1 \).
